title: Add support for `podman ps --latest` on windows
body:
### Feature request description

Was testing out .NET Aspire on windows and trying to get it to work with podman vs docker. It looks like they depend on this command as a health check: https://github.com/dotnet/aspire/blob/70ea605b6d46be7afc16748ec4b5d037439857f0/src/Aspire.Hosting/Dcp/DcpHostService.cs#L191

`podman ps --latest` doesn't seem to work on windows:

```cmd
>podman -v
podman version 4.9.0

>podman ps --latest
Error: unknown flag: --latest
See 'podman ps --help'
```

In the wsl2 machine it looks like its supported
```bash
$ podman -v
podman version 4.8.3

$ podman ps -h
List containers

Description:
  Prints out information about the containers

Usage:
  podman ps [options]

Examples:
  podman ps -a
  podman ps -a --format "{{.ID}}  {{.Image}}  {{.Labels}}  {{.Mounts}}"
  podman ps --size --sort names

Options:
  -a, --all                  Show all the containers, default is only running containers
      --external             Show containers in storage not controlled by Podman
  -f, --filter stringArray   Filter output based on conditions given
      --format string        Pretty-print containers to JSON or using a Go template
  -n, --last int             Print the n last created containers (all states) (default -1)
  -l, --latest               Act on the latest container podman is aware of     <----------------------------
                             Not supported with the "--remote" flag
      --no-trunc             Display the extended information
      --noheading            Do not print headers
      --ns                   Display namespace information
  -p, --pod                  Print the ID and name of the pod the containers are associated with
  -q, --quiet                Print the numeric IDs of the containers only
  -s, --size                 Display the total file sizes
      --sort choice          Sort output by: command, created, id, image, names, runningfor, size, status
      --sync                 Sync container state with OCI runtime
  -w, --watch uint           Watch the ps output on an interval in seconds
```

### Suggest potential solution

Add support for the `--latest` flag. Is there a technical reason this flag isn't supported on windows?

### Have you considered any alternatives?

A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body:
We do not support the --latest flag on any --remote system including Mac and Windows.

There is a fix in upstream to remove the --latest examples from help.

```
$ ./bin/podman-remote ps --help
List containers

Description:
  Prints out information about the containers

Usage:
  podman-remote ps [options]

Examples:
  podman ps -a
  podman ps -a --format "{{.ID}}  {{.Image}}  {{.Labels}}  {{.Mounts}}"
  podman ps --size --sort names

Options:
  -a, --all                  Show all the containers, default is only running containers
      --external             Show containers in storage not controlled by Podman
  -f, --filter stringArray   Filter output based on conditions given
      --format string        Pretty-print containers to JSON or using a Go template
  -n, --last int             Print the n last created containers (all states) (default -1)
      --no-trunc             Display the extended information
      --noheading            Do not print headers
      --ns                   Display namespace information
  -p, --pod                  Print the ID and name of the pod the containers are associated with
  -q, --quiet                Print the numeric IDs of the containers only
  -s, --size                 Display the total file sizes
      --sort choice          Sort output by: command, created, id, image, names, runningfor, size, status
      --sync                 Sync container state with OCI runtime
  -w, --watch uint           Watch the ps output on an interval in seconds
```
